lines.en_lit: ritual impurity, the beginning is fit to override ritual impurity, its end also overrides Shabbat, the beginning is not fit to override its end also does not override
not difficult to the Rav Ḥisda, he does not hold its end is like its beginning. Shabbat in a communal permitted, its end also overrides
ritual impurity, which is overridden in a communal the beginning of which is the essential atonement, overrides its end, which is not the essential atonement, does not override
was stated: one who extinguishes the fire of the coal pan or of the candelabrum, Abaye said: He is liable. Rava said: He is not liable.

where one extinguished upon the top of the altar, everyone agrees that he is liable. When they disagree, where he brought down to ground and extinguished Abaye said: He is liable, fire of the altar. Rava said: He is not liable, once it has been removed it is removed
But this that Rav Naḥman said Rabba bar Avuh said: One who takes down a coal from upon the altar and extinguishes it is liable, in accordance with whose in accordance with Abaye?
You can even say is in accordance with Rava There, was not removed to fulfill its mitzva. here, was removed to fulfill its mitzva.

where he brought down to the ground and extinguished everyone agrees that he is not liable. When they disagree where one extinguished upon the top of the altar. Abaye said: He is liable, fire of the altar. Rava said: He is not liable, once it has been removed it is removed

But this that Rav Naḥman said Rabba bar Avuh said: He who takes down a coal from upon the altar and extinguishes it is liable, in accordance with whose neither in accordance with Abaye, nor in accordance Rava. There, was not removed to fulfill its mitzva; here, was removed to fulfill its mitzva,
